channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Delhi Woman Escapes Assault by Hiding in Uttarakhand Forest Overnight
item.description: A 31-year-old woman from Delhi faced a terrifying ordeal during her solo trip to Uttarakhand. After arriving in Kathgodam on March 5, 2026, she hired a taxi to Nainital. At around 1:30 a.m., the drive...

item.title: Geopolitical Tensions Highlight Fragility of Global Oil Supply Chains
item.description: The recent escalation of conflict involving Iran, the US and Israel has put the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime corridor for oil transport, under severe strain. This strait connects Persian Gulf...

item.title: Convicted UK child killer Ian Huntley dies following prison assault
item.description: Ian Huntley, the British man convicted in 2003 for the murders of 10-year-old girls Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman, has died at 52 after being attacked in Frankland prison in north-east England. Hunt...

item.title: Suliman Carrim Refuses Private Testimony at Madlanga Commission Despite Threats
item.description: Businessman and ANC member Suliman Carrim appeared before the Madlanga Commission, which is investigating allegations of corruption, political interference, and crime within South Africa's justice sys...

item.title: North Unguja Entrepreneurs Encouraged to Apply Business Training for Enterprise Growth and Economic Empowerment
item.description: In North Unguja, Zanzibar, entrepreneurs have been strongly encouraged to utilise the business skills they acquired from a recent training programme to improve their businesses and gain greater econom...

item.title: Government urged to provide support to sugar industry amid Middle East crisis
item.description: The Confederation of Sugar Producers Association Inc. (Confed) has urged the Philippine government to help lessen the effects of the Middle East crisis on the local sugar industry. The group recommend...

item.title: Government Launches Coordinated Operations to Tackle Illegal Mining and Corruption
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile has confirmed that the South African government is intensifying intelligence-led operations to combat illegal mining in Gauteng, particularly in the East and West Rand ...

item.title: Trump Praises and Imitates Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum's Voice During Summit Remarks on Cartels

item.title: Lagos advises Sarah Martins to carry out charity in approved locations
item.description: Lagos State Commissioner for Environment and Water Resources, Tokunbo Wahab, don issue warning to actress Sarah Martins about her street cooking activities. Even though her intentions na for charity, ...

item.title: English is secure in New Zealand, but heritage languages need support
item.description: The recently introduced English Language Bill in New Zealand has sparked debate, although English is already the dominant language, spoken by 96% of the population and prevalent across media, educatio...

item.title: Atiku’s Team Accuses Bwala Of Political Opportunism After Al Jazeera Interview
item.description: The media office of former Vice President Atiku Abubakar don come out sharply against Daniel Bwala, wey be Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Policy Communication. According to statement from...

item.title: Addressing the Needs of Single Employees in Family-Focused Workplaces
item.description: Modern workplaces have increasingly adapted to support dual-career couples and working parents by providing flexible hours, paid parental leave, and childcare subsidies. While these measures are benef...

item.title: Calgary hosts gala to support STARS Air Ambulance funding
item.description: Calgary is set to host a fundraising gala to support STARS Air Ambulance, with a goal of raising $1.7 million. STARS (Shock Trauma Air Rescue Service) provides critical air ambulance services across A...

item.title: The Capture Season Three: Evaluating Facial Recognition and Deepfake Technology Accuracy
item.description: The third season of BBC’s conspiracy thriller drama The Capture continues to explore themes of facial recognition and deepfake AI technology. The series portrays investigators using facial recognition...

item.title: Youtuber Anurag Dobhal involved in high-speed crash while livestreaming
item.description: A disturbing incident reportedly took place involving popular YouTuber Anurag Dobhal, who was livestreaming while driving at dangerously high speed, reaching up to 150 kmph. According to the available...

item.title: Metro Manila Launches Free Electric Love Bus Routes for Commuters
item.description: Metro Manila commuters will soon enjoy free rides on electric 'Love Buses' along key routes in Quezon City and Pasig City. This initiative is a collaboration between the Metropolitan Manila Developmen...

item.title: Seven Small Oil Refineries Helping Communities Maintain Local Fuel Supply
item.description: Oil refineries na places wey dem dey take crude oil turn into different fuels like petrol, diesel, kerosene, and lubricants. Normally, big refineries dey handle hundreds of thousands of barrels every ...

item.title: Kenyan Celebrities and Their Ongoing Mansion Projects
item.description: In Kenya, some celebrities have taken their financial success into building luxurious homes, giving their fans a glimpse of their journey from modest beginnings to owning palatial properties. Comedian...

item.title: Government doctors denied permission for hunger strike over pay demands
item.description: The Legal Coordination Committee (LCC) representing government doctors has strongly criticised the authorities for denying permission to conduct a hunger strike at the Rajiv Gandhi Government General ...

item.title: Gateway Airport to Boost Cargo Movement Across Nigeria and West Africa
item.description: The Nigeria Shippers’ Council CEO, Akutah Pius Okeyima, don talk say the Gateway International Airport na very important infrastructure wey fit solve the wahala wey dey cargo movement for Nigeria and ...
